rules.title: Past Tense Formation - Basic Structure
rules.text: Slovak past tense is formed using the past participle (l-participle) + present tense of 'byť' (to be) as auxiliary. The participle agrees with gender and number: -l (masculine singular), -la (feminine singular), -lo (neuter singular), -li (masculine plural), -ly (feminine/neuter plural).
examples: robil som – I did (masculine speaker)
robila som – I did (feminine speaker)
robili sme – we did

rules.title: Past Tense Conjugation - 'robiť' (to do)
rules.text: Full conjugation table for imperfective verb 'robiť':
examples: Ja som robil domácu úlohu – I did homework (masc.)
Ty si robila chybu – You made a mistake (fem.)
Oni robili projekt – They did a project

rules.title: Past Tense Conjugation - 'napísať' (to write - perfective)
rules.text: Full conjugation table for perfective verb 'napísať':
examples: Napísal som list – I wrote a letter (completed)
Napísala si email – You wrote an email (completed)
Napísali sme knihu – We wrote a book (completed)

rules.title: Auxiliary Verb 'byť' - Often Omitted in 3rd Person
rules.text: In 3rd person (on, ona, ono, oni, ony), the auxiliary 'bol/bola/bolo/boli' is often omitted in standard speech. It's included for emphasis or clarity.
examples: On robil – He did (standard)
On bol robil – He did (emphatic)
Ona čítala – She read
Oni pracovali – They worked

rules.title: Reflexive Verbs in Past Tense
rules.text: Reflexive verbs use 'sa' or 'si' with past tense. The reflexive particle comes after the auxiliary verb (or after the participle in 3rd person). Word order: subject + auxiliary + reflexive + participle.
examples: Učil som sa – I learned (masc.)
Učila som sa – I learned (fem.)
Umyl si sa – You washed yourself (masc.)
Umyla si sa – You washed yourself (fem.)
Učil sa – He learned
Učili sme sa – We learned

rules.title: Reflexive Conjugation Table - 'učiť sa' (to learn)
examples: Učil som sa slovenčinu – I learned Slovak (masc.)
Učila si sa celý deň – You studied all day (fem.)
Učili sa spolu – They studied together

rules.title: Negation in Past Tense
rules.text: Add 'ne-' prefix to the participle. The auxiliary verb remains unchanged. In 3rd person, you can also negate the omitted auxiliary (nebol/nebola/neboli).
examples: Nerobil som – I didn't do (masc.)
Nerobila som – I didn't do (fem.)
Nerobil si – You didn't do (masc.)
Nerobil – He didn't do
Nerobili sme – We didn't do

rules.title: Negation Table - 'robiť' (to do)
examples: Nevidel som ho – I didn't see him
Nepočula si ma – You didn't hear me (fem.)
Nepracovali včera – They didn't work yesterday

rules.title: Negation with Reflexives
rules.text: With reflexive verbs, negate the participle with 'ne-' prefix. The reflexive particle position remains the same.
examples: Neučil som sa – I didn't learn (masc.)
Neučila som sa – I didn't learn (fem.)
Neumyl si sa – You didn't wash yourself
Neučili sme sa – We didn't learn

rules.title: Aspect in Past Tense - Imperfective
rules.text: Imperfective verbs in past tense describe ongoing, repeated, or habitual actions in the past without focus on completion. They answer 'what was happening' or 'what used to happen'.
examples: Čítal som knihu – I was reading a book (ongoing)
Pracoval každý deň – He worked every day (habitual)
Učili sme sa slovenčinu – We were learning Slovak (process)

rules.title: Aspect in Past Tense - Perfective
rules.text: Perfective verbs in past tense describe completed actions with a clear result or endpoint. They answer 'what happened' or 'what got done'.
examples: Prečítal som knihu – I read the book (finished it)
Napísal list – He wrote a letter (completed)
Kúpili sme auto – We bought a car (completed action)

rules.title: Aspect Pairs in Past Tense
rules.text: Most verbs come in aspect pairs. Use imperfective for process/duration, perfective for completion. Both use the same past tense formation rules.
examples: Písal som (imp.) – I was writing
Napísal som (perf.) – I wrote/finished writing
Jedol som (imp.) – I was eating
Zjedol som (perf.) – I ate it all up

rules.title: Irregular Past Participle - 'ísť' (to go)
rules.text: The verb 'ísť' has irregular past participle 'išiel/išla/išlo/išli'.
examples: Išiel som domov – I went home (masc.)
Išla som do školy – I went to school (fem.)
Išli sme na prechádzku – We went for a walk

rules.title: Irregular Past Participle - 'jesť' (to eat)
rules.text: The verb 'jesť' has irregular past participle 'jedol/jedla/jedlo/jedli'.
examples: Jedol som obed – I ate lunch (masc.)
Jedla som večeru – I ate dinner (fem.)
Jedli sme pizzu – We ate pizza

rules.title: Irregular Past Participle - 'piť' (to drink)
rules.text: The verb 'piť' has irregular past participle 'pil/pila/pilo/pili'.
examples: Pil som vodu – I drank water (masc.)
Pila som kávu – I drank coffee (fem.)
Pili sme čaj – We drank tea

rules.title: Irregular Past Participle - 'byť' (to be)
rules.text: The verb 'byť' has irregular past participle 'bol/bola/bolo/boli'.
examples: Bol som doma – I was at home (masc.)
Bola som unavená – I was tired (fem.)
Boli sme šťastní – We were happy

rules.title: Irregular Past Participle - 'môcť' (can/to be able)
rules.text: The verb 'môcť' has irregular past participle 'mohol/mohla/mohlo/mohli'.
examples: Mohol som prísť – I could come (masc.)
Mohla som pomôcť – I could help (fem.)
Mohli sme zostať – We could stay

rules.title: Irregular Verbs - 'vedieť' (to know)
examples: Vedel som odpoveď – I knew the answer (masc.)
Vedela som pravdu – I knew the truth (fem.)
Vedeli sme to – We knew it

rules.title: Irregular Verbs - Consonant Changes
rules.text: Some verbs undergo consonant changes in past participle: niesť→niesol, viesť→viedol, piecť→piekol. These follow regular conjugation patterns but with modified stems.
examples: Niesol som tašku – I carried a bag (masc.)
Viedla som skupinu – I led a group (fem.)
Piekli sme koláč – We baked a cake
